pattern: Once every access time is converted to minutes and grouped per person, flagging someone is just checking whether any three of their sorted times fit inside a 60-minute window.
hints: Group the access times by employee name, and convert each 'HHMM' string into a single minutes-since-midnight number so times are easy to compare.
Sort each employee's minute values, then slide a window of 3 consecutive sorted times looking for a case where the last minus the first is under 60.
Only the exact difference matters -- a gap of exactly 60 minutes does not count as the same one-hour period, so use a strict less-than comparison.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, two employees flagged
statement example three, times given out of order
single access time, cannot be high-access
three accesses exactly 60 minutes apart, does not count
midnight wraparound is not treated as adjacent

stress.description: random employees with random clustered access times
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    names = ['emp' + str(i) for i in range(10)]
    access = []
    for _ in range(n):
        name = rng.choice(names)
        h = rng.randint(0, 23)
        m = rng.randint(0, 59)
        access.append([name, f'{h:02d}{m:02d}'])
    return [access]
